column1: Upon reading the note, you realize it is a recipe for "Frog Legs Fricassee".
column2: 看完紙條後，你發現這是份「炸青蛙腿」的食譜。

column1: Look pets... we have a visitor!
column2: 「看啊，寵物…我們來客人了！」

column1: Powers of Night, Shadows of Day -- Heed now my Words! Henceforth you STAY!
column2: 「夜晚之力，白晝之影——聽我話！從此你留下！」

column1: Well Dearies!  What shall we have for supper today?
column2: 「好啊，親愛的們！今晚吃什麼？」

column1: Hero sandwiches? I had something more formal in mind.
column2: 「英雄三明治？我想要點更正式的。」

column1: Now how does that spell go...?
column2: 「那咒語要怎麼唸…？」

column1: Hear me, oh Powers Of Klatha and Mana! Turn now my guest Into species called Rana!
column2: 「聽我說啊，克拉瑟和法力之力！把我的客人變成蛙族！」

column1: I learned that spell from Erasmus, kids.  Doesn't it look delicious?
column2: 「我從伊拉斯謨那裡學的咒語，孩子們。看起來美味嗎？」

column1: BE STILL!
column2: 「別動！」

column1: Critics!  We can't all be gourmands, I suppose.
column2: 「美食家呢！我們也不是都是美食家。」

column1: So you're the one who's trying to be a hero around here.  The only good hero's a dead hero, I always say!
column2: 「所以你就是想在這裡逞英雄的傢伙。我常說，好英雄就是死英雄！」

column1: But I do have a need for a brave fool.  Are YOU brave?
column2: 但我確實需要個勇敢的傻瓜。你勇敢嗎？

column1: Yes
column2: 是的

column1: No
column2: 不是

column1: You make little froggy sounds, trying your best to indicate your agreement.
column2: 你發出小青蛙的叫聲，盡力表示同意。

column1: Wellll...
If you're willing to do a small little teensy favor for me, I might reconsider having you for supper.
column2: 「如果你願意幫我做個小小的小忙，我也許會重新考慮把你當晚餐吃。」

column1: I need the root of a mandrake plant that grows in the graveyard.  Will you be a sweet and fetch me some?
column2: 「我需要在墓園裡長的曼陀羅根。親愛的，你能去給我拿一些嗎？」

column1: Anything but Frog Legs Fricassee!, you think. You croak your agreement to the task.
column2: 「什麼都好，就別是炸青蛙腿！」你想著。你用呱呱聲表示同意這任務。

column1: And I had my mouth watering for frog.  Oh well!
column2: 「我本來還滿想吃青蛙呢。算了！」

column1: Hear what I say, And hear me right: Mandrake must be pulled At precisely Midnight!
column2: 「聽好我的話，聽清楚：曼陀羅要在午夜時分拔起！」

column1: This I tell you And this I say: Return with the root 'Ere the break of next day.
column2: 「這是我告訴你的，我這樣說：天亮前帶著根回來。」

column1: Hear what I say And know I don't lie: Bring back the root Or else you will die!!
column2: 「聽我說，相信我不會說謊：把根帶回來，不然你會死！」

column1: You, Shoo!
Well, you won't be so tough after you've simmered in a little wine sauce.
column2: 「好啦，你在紅酒醬裡燉一下就不會那麼硬了。」

column1: Bon appetite, Dearies!
column2: 「好胃口，親愛的們！」

column1: It's just as well.  I'd lose my dinner if you were brave.
column2: 「也好啦。你要是勇敢的話我就沒胃口了。」

column1: Nothin' says lovin' like frog legs in the oven.
column2: 「什麼都比不上烤青蛙腿能表達愛意。」

column1: No mandrake for me, eh?  What a waste of spells you turned out to be.
column2: 「沒有曼陀羅給我？你這浪費法術的傢伙。」

column1: Actually, since it's my breakfast time, we'll have some amphibian omelette with bacon and legs.
column2: 「實際上，既然現在是我吃早餐的時間，我們就吃炒兩棲動物加培根和腿吧。」

column1: You again?
column2: 「又是你？」

column1: Hello, Hero.
column2: 「你好，英雄。」

column1: Powers that rule Over regions soggy: Change this creature Back into a froggy!
column2: 「統治濕潤地域的力量：把這生物變回青蛙！」

column1: Ooooh... yummy!  I'm glad you returned.
column2: 「喔…好吃！我很高興你回來了。」

column1: What have you DONE to me? How DARE you use my own spell against me? I'll, I'll... Oh, oh!
column2: 「你對我做了什麼？你敢用我自己的咒語對付我？我會…我會…哦，哦！」

column1: Hear me, O Elements of Air and Wind. Give me the power to save my own skin!
column2: 「聽我說，空氣與風的元素。給我拯救我自己的力量！」

column1: Back so soon?
column2: 「這麼快就回來？」

column1: Ah!  Here it is.
column2: 「啊！找到了。」

column1: Spirits of Mist And Creatures of Bog: Transform my guest To the shape of a Frog.
column2: 「迷霧之精與沼澤生物：把我的客人變成青蛙的形狀。」

column1: Yum, Yum.  Froggie Frappe!
column2: 「好吃，好吃。青蛙小冰沙！」

column1: Did you bring me my mandrake like you promised?
column2: 「你有像約好的那樣給我帶曼陀羅來嗎？」

column1: Well?  Where is it?
column2: 「好？在哪？」

column1: What's the matter?  Got a frog in your throat?
column2: 「怎麼了？喉嚨卡了隻青蛙？」

column1: I suppose I'll have to turn you back into whatever it is that you were. Pity!  You're much more appetizing this way.
column2: 「我想我得把你變回原本的樣子。可惜了！你這樣看起來美味多了。」

column1: Creatures of Bog And Spirits of Fog: Return the true form To this rather dumb Frog.
column2: 「沼澤生物與霧靈：把真實的形態還給這隻相當笨的青蛙。」

column1: Now, did you put it in your backpack?
column2: 「那，你把它放進背包裡了嗎？」

column1: You try your best to croak out your name, or at least let the witch know that you do indeed have one.
column2: 你盡力用呱呱聲說出你的名字，至少要讓女巫知道你確實有個名字。

column1: As you make a feeble croaking sound, you try to nod your head.
column2: 當你發出微弱的呱呱聲時，你試著點了點頭。

column1: Ah...that's it! Frog Legs Fricassee.
column2: 「啊…那就是了！炸青蛙腿。」

column1: Once again you're a frog, and once again you can't move.  You find it very exasperating!
column2: 你再次變成了青蛙，再次動不了。你覺得這很惱人！

column1: Your body is frozen by the power of the witch's spell.
column2: 你的身體被女巫的咒語凍住了。

column1: The witch is hopping mad.
column2: 女巫氣得跳腳。

column1: Lovies, it's about time we dine.
column2: 「親愛的們，該吃飯了。」

column1: Kids!  We have it!
column2: 「孩子！我們拿到了！」

column1: That's it!  The final ingredient.  Now we can make our greatest creation...
column2: 「就是它！最後的材料。現在我們可以製作最偉大的創作…」

column1: MANDRAKE MOUSSE!
column2: 「曼陀羅慕斯！」

column1: What's that, children?  You think we should reward our lackey here?
column2: 「怎麼了，孩子？你們認為我們應該獎勵這小嘍囉？」

column1: Very well, ex-frog.  I'll let you live this time.  Next time, though, it's frog legs for sure!
column2: 「很好，前青蛙。這次我饒了你。不過下次，一定是青蛙腿！」

column1: So... Go!
column2: 「所以…滾！」

column1: Stubborn, aren't you?
column2: 「你很固執呢？」

column1: Now, Oh-Soon-To-Be-Supper... I don't suppose you have a name?
column2: 現在，唉呀就要變晚餐啦…我猜你沒有名字？

column1: Well, if you won't be a sweet, then you'll be my meat!
column2: 「好吧，如果你不乖，就變成我的肉！」

column1: This I vow: Stay there now!
column2: 「這是我發誓的：現在待著！」

column1: Who would have guessed that little chicken hut would have this much room in it.
column2: 誰會猜到那個小雞腳小屋裡面會有這麼大的空間。

column1: You make a note to yourself to get the name of Baba Yaga's interior decorator.
column2: 你在心裡記下要去問芭芭雅嘎的室內設計師的名字。

column1: The floor is tidy, with a pile of straw off the screen in one corner.
column2: 地板很整潔，角落裡有堆稻草在螢幕外。

column1: A quick look around shows that this is a creepy place, despite the pot -- or is it a cauldron? -- simmering briskly on the fire.  Look at the size of that spider!
column2: 快速掃一眼就知道這是個詭異的地方，儘管鍋子——或者說是大鍋？——在火上咕嘟咕嘟地煮著。看看那隻蜘蛛的大小！

column1: The grisly remains of some man-like animal.
column2: 某種人形生物的可怕遺骨。

column1: Owning this skull helps Baba Yaga keep a head of the game.
column2: 擁有這骷髏幫助芭芭雅嘎走在遊戲的前面。

column1: Is that a SMILE on that spider's face?
column2: 那隻蜘蛛的臉上有微笑嗎？

column1: You can almost read the spider's thoughts:  "Yes!  Yes!  Get your hand over here so I can eat!"
column2: 你幾乎可以讀出蜘蛛的想法：「是的！是的！把你的手放過來我才能吃！」

column1: Step into my parlor!
column2: 踏進我的起居室吧！

column1: The kitchen knives on the table look old but sharp.  You can't quite make out what is in the bottles.
column2: 桌上的廚房刀看起來又舊又銳。你看不太清楚瓶子裡裝著什麼。

column1: Isn't that a telephone wire insulator?
column2: 那不是電話線絕緣體嗎？

column1: You HATE houses with cobwebs in the corners, especially when there's an immense (and hungry-looking) spider squatting cheerily in the middle of the cobweb.
column2: 你討厭角落有蜘蛛網的房子，特別是當有隻巨大的（且看起來很餓的）蜘蛛高興地蹲在蜘蛛網中央的時候。

column1: You have to get there, first.
column2: 你必須先到達那裡。

column1: You have second thoughts, and you decide not to attempt such a potentially dangerous excursion.
column2: 你改變了主意，決定不嘗試這樣可能很危險的遠征。

column1: What's to climb?
column2: 有什麼好爬的？

column1: Those are the biggest chicken legs you've ever seen.
column2: 那是你見過最大的雞腿。

column1: The green slime dripping off of the fence top makes you realize that climbing is not a good idea.
column2: 從柵欄頂端滴下來的綠色粘液讓你意識到攀爬不是個好主意。

column1: The sharp ends of the fence are covered with some nasty-looking green slime.
column2: 柵欄的尖端被一些令人厭惡的綠色粘液覆蓋。

column1: This stuff reminds you of the slime eating the barstool away at the tavern.
column2: 這東西讓你想起在酒館裡吃掉酒吧凳子的那些粘液。

column1: Of course, most fences aren't poisoned on top!
column2: 當然，大多數的柵欄頂端都不會有毒！

column1: You've seen one, you've seen them all, I say.
column2: 我說，你看過一個，就等於看過所有的。

column1: Baba Yaga is the most powerful Ogress around.  If you have any brains, you'll stay away from her.
column2: 「芭芭雅嘎是這一帶最強大的女食人妖。如果你有腦子，就別靠近她。」

column1: All the other skulls have glowing eyes.  But me?... noooo!  I don't need lovely glowing eyes!  Just open and close the gate, that's all I'm good for.
column2: 「所有其他的骷髏都有發光的眼睛。但我？…不要！我不需要漂亮的發光眼睛！只要開關門，這就是我唯一的用處。」

column1: But if you can give me a glowing gem for my eyes, then I'll let you in the gate.
column2: 「但如果你能給我個發光的寶石當眼睛，我就放你進門。」

column1: All I ask is for a little gem or jewel that glows in the dark. You're the big hero, so go find one!
column2: 「我要的就是在黑暗中發光的小寶石或珠寶。你是大英雄，去找一個吧！」

column1: Baba Yaga's hut will squat if you say the rhyme.
column2: 「如果你說出那句韻文，芭芭雅嘎的小屋就會蹲下。」

column1: You're some hero if you don't even know what an ogre is.
column2: 「你要是連食人妖是什麼都不知道，就太不像英雄了。」

column1: The hut will squat if you say, 'Hut of brown, Now sit down.'
column2: 「如果你說『棕色的小屋，現在坐下』，小屋就會蹲下。」

column1: Those bone brains are Baba Yaga's spies, so they get glowing eyes.
column2: 「那些骨頭腦子是芭芭雅嘎的間諜，所以他們有發光的眼睛。」

column1: Right!
column2: 沒錯！

column1: I won't let you in until I get a gem so I can see!
column2: 「在我得到寶石能看見之前，我不會放你進去！」

column1: I don't need the fur.  I think the 'bald look' gives me a certain distinction, don't you?
column2: 「我不需要毛髮。我覺得『禿頭造型』給了我某種風格，你說對不？」

column1: The gate's gone.
column2: 門消失了。

column1: The eyes of the large skull on the gate flash merrily away at you.
column2: 門上大骷髏的眼睛朝你閃閃發光。

column1: The large skull on the gate seems to stare vacantly at you.
column2: 門上的大骷髏似乎茫茫然地盯著你。

column1: This place already looks like a nightmare.  Eating magic mushrooms would likely make it even worse.
column2: 這個地方看起來已經像噩夢了。吃魔法蘑菇可能會讓它更糟。

column1: Oh, the rhyme.  Yeah, you're right, that was part of the deal.  O.k., the rhyme is 'Hut of Brown, Now Sit Down'.
column2: 「哦，韻文。是啊，你說得對，那是協議的一部分。好吧，韻文是『棕色小屋，現在坐下』。」

column1: Baba Yaga
column2: 芭芭雅嘎

column1: Deal
column2: 協議

column1: Gem
column2: 寶石

column1: Hut
column2: 小屋

column1: Ogress
column2: 女食人妖

column1: Rhyme
column2: 韻文

column1: Skulls
column2: 骷髏

column1: The door is open, but hardly inviting.
column2: 門是開著的，但不太吸引人。

column1: The door of the hut is ornately decorated.
column2: 小屋的門裝飾得很華麗。

column1: The hut is a strange little house perched on chicken legs.
column2: 這是個奇怪的小房子，站在雞腳上。

column1: The hut does not respond while the gate is closed.
column2: 當門關著時，小屋沒有反應。

column1: Nothing happens.
column2: 什麼都沒發生。

column1: You hear a voice in your head asking, 'What is the rhyme?'
column2: 你聽到腦子裡傳來的聲音問著，『韻文是什麼？』

column1: Looking around, you get the feeling that this is not a very friendly place to be.
column2: 四周張望，你感覺這不是個很友善的地方。

column1: You place the glowing gem inside the skull.
column2: 你把發光的寶石放進骷髏裡。

column1: I can see!  I have eyes again!!
column2: 「我能看見了！我又有眼睛了！」

column1: Yeccchhh! Is THAT what you look like???
column2: 「哎呀！你看起來就是這樣？」

column1: Oh well!  Have fun visiting Baba Yaga.  And good luck....you'll need it!
column2: 「好吧！祝訪問芭芭雅嘎愉快。祝你好運…你會需要的！」

column1: There goes Baba Yaga.  Now you've REALLY made her soar!
column2: 芭芭雅嘎走了。現在你真的讓她飆起來了！

column1: So you made it back, did you?  She is expecting you.
column2: 「所以你回來了？她在等你。」

column1: What an awful creature, to turn me into a FROG!, you say to yourself. Someday, I must return the favor.
column2: 多麼可怕的生物，竟然把我變成青蛙！你心想。有一天，我一定要報這個仇。

column1: You tell a rhyme about:
column2: 你講了個關於下列的韻文：

column1: Beauty
column2: 美麗

column1: The alien From Venus
column2: 來自金星的外星人

column1: Hut of Brown
column2: 棕色小屋

column1: Well, is it a deal or isn't it?
column2: 好，這是協議還是不是？

column1: I'm not sure
column2: 我不確定

column1: Yes
column2: 是的

column1: No
column2: 不是

column1: Did you bring me my glowing gem?
column2: 「你給我帶發光的寶石了嗎？」
